title: Quadrant Hypothesis
description: A prediction derived from an Ofman quadrant: given an observed core quality, hypothesise the likely pitfall, the balancing challenge, and the allergy (what the person is most likely to be irritated by in others). Conditional, probabilistic, falsifiable, plural. Never a diagnosis or a personality verdict.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the human-mind schema family this document conforms to.

quality.description: English quality id this hypothesis is about.

from.description: The observation(s) this hypothesis derives from. Traceability is required — a prediction with no source observation is not allowed.

observationRef.description: Identifier of the source quality observation.

confidence.description: Confidence in the source observation. Caps every prediction confidence below.

predictions.description: Competing hypotheses about quadrant positions and likely reactions. Confidences need not sum to 1.

position.description: Which quadrant corner this prediction concerns.

response.description: The likely disposition or reaction, phrased conditionally.

confidence.description: Confidence in this prediction. Must be <= the source observation confidence in 'from'.

falsifier.description: A later observation that would disconfirm this prediction.
